MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Ferlin P. Ruiz, Esq. of TUCKER ELLIS & WEST (415-617-2222), appearing for defendant / moving party Pfizer, Inc.
Stephen Tigerman, Esq. of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN (415-788-1588) for plaintiffs / opposing parties George Michael Sotcan, et al.

RULING:
Defendant Pfizer, Inc.'s motion to change venue on the ground of improper venue. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Pfizer, Inc.'s motion to change venue is denied. The motion to change venue on the ground of convenience of the witnesses and ends of justice is continued to October 6, 2010 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220. Plaintiffs' motion to strike the declarations submitted with the reply papers is denied. Venue is proper in this county because at least one defendant resides in San Francisco County. To facilitate the determination of whether defendant's motion should be granted based on convenience of the witnesses and ends of justice, plaintiffs are required to disclose no later than September 30 the names and last known addresses of each person presently known to plaintiffs who has knowledge of any facts supporting plaintiffs' claims against each defendant and the facts known by that person. The prohibition on ex parte contacts with a plaintiff's physicians does not extend to contacts regarding matters other than the plaintiff's physical condition such as convenience of the location of testifying at trial. Plaintiffs may file supplemental opposition papers no later than September 30. Defendant may file supplemental reply papers no later than October 4. Same ruling as to all joinders. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
